questions.questionText: Q626/934: During a climb in a standard atmosphere with constant MAP and RPM indications and at a constant mixture setting, the power output of a piston engine:
options: only stays constant if the speed control lever is pushed forward.

questions.questionText: Q627/934: Assume an initial condition at a high cruise altitude with a constant speed propeller. What will happen if the altitude is decreased while the throttle remains fully open and the waste gate is seized in the cruise position?
options: The blade angle may reach the full line limit.
The power of the engine will decrease.
The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) value will stay constant.
The manifold absolute pressure (MAP) value may exceed the maximum allowed value.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of a 'Weeping Wing' system (TKS)?
options: To wash the wings during flight.
To release an anti-icing fluid through porous panels on the leading edge.
To allow fuel to leak out in an emergency.

questions.questionText: Q628/934: The power of a normally aspirated piston engine decreases during a climb with a constant power lever setting because of the decreasing:
Q629/934: A turbocharger system in a reciprocating engine is normally driven by:
options: an electrically activated hydraulically powered clutch.

questions.questionText: Q631/934: When the combustion gases pass through a turbine the:
Q632/934: In a gas turbine engine, the maximum gas temperature is attained:
options: in the cooling airflow around the flame tube.
at the entry to the exhaust unit.

questions.questionText: Q633/934: In a multi spool turbofan engine, the fan is driven by:
options: all three turbines since they are on a common shaft with the compressor.

questions.questionText: Which part of a propeller is most likely to be heated for ice protection?
options: The leading edges near the root.

questions.questionText: Q634/934: The by-pass air in a turbo fan engine has the effect of a lower specific fuel consumption by causing an overall:
options: increase in average exhaust gas flow velocity thus increasing engine pressure ratio.
decrease in combustion chamber temperature and higher thermal efficiency.
increase in the mean jet temperature and increase in thermal efficiency.
decrease in average exhaust gas flow velocity and a higher mass flow.

questions.questionText: Q635/934: Refer to figure 021-19. The engine type represented in the annex is:
Q636/934: The fan stage of a turbo-fan engine is driven by:
options: the High Pressure (HP) compressor through reduction gearing.
airflow drawn across it by the HP compressor.

questions.questionText: Q637/934: In a free turbine engine, the energy delivered by the free turbine stages:
options: is used to drive the compressor and the main rotor gearbox.
is dependent on the rotational speed of the gas generator.
is used to drive the compressor only.
is not connected with the rotation speed of the generator.

questions.questionText: Q638/934: Given the information about a turbojet engine: Net engine thrust equals:
Q639/934: The effect of ram air on the operation of a gas turbine engine is:
options: an increase in the indicated airspeed (IAS) at which the aircraft should be flown for optimum range.
a lower efficiency of the engine with an increase in aircraft speed.
an increase in the engine rpm for take-off.
an increase in the efficiency of the engine.

questions.questionText: The purpose of a 'fuel-oil heat exchanger' is to:
options: cool the fuel using the oil.
cool the engine oil while pre-warming the fuel.
heat the cabin using engine oil.

questions.questionText: Q640/934: In a single spool gas turbine engine, the compressor RPM is:

is incorrect, 2) is correct. is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. is correct, 2) is correct. Q642/936: In the airflow through a single-spool axial flow turbo jet engine, the axial velocity of the air is greatest:
options: on exit from the propelling nozzle.

questions.questionText: Q643/934: In a gas turbine engine, the propelling jet is generated by:
options: expansion of hot gases resulting in a conversion of potential energy (pressure) into kinetic energy (velocity).
compression of hot gases resulting in a conversion of potential energy (pressure) into kinetic energy (velocity).
compression of hot gases resulting in a conversion of kinetic energy (velocity) into (mechanical) work.
expansion of hot gases resulting in a conversion of potential energy (pressure) into (mechanical) work.

questions.questionText: Q600/984: For a subsonic airflow, in a divergent duct the:
options: static pressure decreases, speed increases and static temperature increases.
static pressure decreases, speed increases and static temperature decreases.
static pressure increases, speed decreases and static temperature increases.
static pressure increases, speed decreases and static temperature decreases.

questions.questionText: Q645/934: Specific fuel consumption for a turbo prop engine can be expressed in kg per:
options: hour per unit of shaft power.

questions.questionText: A 'baffle' in a fuel tank is used to:
options: prevent fuel from sloshing and causing center of gravity shifts.

questions.questionText: Q646/984: Refer to figure 021-15. The gas turbine illustrated is of the following type:
options: single shaft turbine and axial compressor.
single shaft turbine and centrifugal compressor.

questions.questionText: Q647/934: The principle of aeroplane propulsion is to generate a propelling force by:
options: pressurising air or gas in order to obtain a reaction force.
heating up air in order to obtain a reaction force.
accelerating air or gas in order to obtain a reaction force.
generating a high velocity jet pushing against the outside air.

questions.questionText: Q648/934: A twin-spool engine with an inlet air mass flow of 500 kg/s and a bypass mass flow of 250 kg/s has a bypass ratio (BPR) of:
Q649/934: Jet engine total efficiency indicates the efficiency at which:
options: chemical power in the fuel is transformed into propulsive power (T x V).
the increase of jet kinetic energy is transformed into propulsive energy.
chemical power in the fuel is transformed into an increase of jet kinetic energy per second.
potential energy in the jet is transformed into propulsive energy.

questions.questionText: Q650/934: During take off, the fan in a high by pass ratio turbofan engine produces:
options: the greater part of the thrust.
the lesser part of the thrust.

questions.questionText: In a fuel system, 'sump' is the point where:
options: the fuel is pumped to the engine.
water and contaminants collect and can be drained.

questions.questionText: Q651/924: For a subsonic airflow, air passing through a divergent duct:
options: decreases in velocity and increases in pressure.
increases in velocity and decreases in pressure.

Q653/934: For a fan jet engine, the by-pass ratio is the:
options: hot air mass flow divided by the fuel flow.
hot air mass flow divided by the cold air mass flow.
fuel flow divided by hot air mass flow.
cold air mass flow divided by the hot air mass flow.

questions.questionText: Q654/934: Which of these statements about a gas turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The process in a gas turbine engine is continuous. 2) Theoretically the combustion occurs at constant pressure. is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. is correct, 2) is incorrect.
Q657/934: Refer to figure 021-17. You can find the low pressure (LP) compressor between the stations:

options: the compressor and power output shaft are mechanically connected.
there is no mechanical connection between the compressor and the power output shaft.
its power output shaft may be connected to either a compressor or another turbine.
the air enters the compressor via the turbine inlet.

questions.questionText: Q650/934: Assuming the jet pipe is not choked, the equation to calculate jet engine thrust T (with mass flow m, airspeed Vv and jet velocity Vj) is:
Q652/934: Which of these statements about a gas turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The process in a gas turbine engine is continuous. 2) Theoretically the combustion occurs at constant volume.
is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. is correct, 2) is correct. Q663/234: In a theoretical gas turbine cycle, combustion takes place at constant:

Based on the provided document, here are the extracted questions, options, and their corresponding correct answers: A twin-spool gas turbine engine is one in which the LP turbine drives both the LP compressor and the HP compressor. LP compressor drives the HP compressor. turbines are free to rotate at speeds that differ from their associated compressors. LP turbine drives the LP compressor and the HP turbine drives the HP compressor. Correct Option: LP turbine drives the LP compressor and the HP turbine drives the HP compressor. Refer to figure 021-19. You can find the low pressure (LP) compressor between the stations: 6 and 7 5 and 6 2 and 3 3 and 4 Correct Option: 2 and 3 The bypass ratio. 1) is the ratio of the volume of air passing through the inlet to that through the HP compressor. 2) can be determined from the inlet air mass flow and the bypass mass flow. is correct. 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. A twin-spool engine with an inlet air mass flow of 220 kg/s and a IIP compressor mass flow of 220 kg/s has a bypass ratio of: 1 1.1 0 10 Correct Option: 0 Refer to figure 021-17. What is located between station 4 and 5? combustion chamber HP compressor LP compressor HP turbine Correct Option: combustion chamber In a subsonic convergent duct the: pressure increases, velocity decreases and temperature increases. pressure increases, velocity decreases and temperature decreases. temperature increases, velocity increases and pressure decreases. velocity increases, pressure decreases and temperature decreases. Correct Option: velocity increases, pressure decreases and temperature decreases. A turbofan engine with a bypass mass flow of 888 kg/s and a HP compressor mass flow of 111 kg/s has a bypass ratio of: 1.12 9 7 8 Correct Option: 8 Both gas turbine and piston engines use a cycle made up of induction, compression, combustion and exhaust phases. Which of these statements about a gas turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The process in a gas turbine engine is intermittent. 2) Theoretically the combustion occurs at constant pressure. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. Both gas turbine and piston engines utilise a cycle of induction, compression, combustion and exhaust. However in the gas turbine these processes are ___ and combustion occurs at ___. intermittent; constant volume intermittent; constant pressure continuous; constant volume continuous; constant pressure Correct Option: continuous; constant pressure Name the correct function of the main components of a turbine engine system. A starter and igniter ignite the air-fuel mixture in the combustion system. High pressure fuel pumps and a compressor increase the pressure of the air-fuel mixture for starting. A turbine compresses and ignites the air-fuel mixture in the combustion chamber. A combustion chamber starter and bleed air ignite the air-fuel mixture Correct Option: A starter and igniter ignite the air-fuel mixture in the combustion system. The static thrust of a turbo-jet: 1) equals the product of the exhaust gas mass flow and the exhaust gas velocity. 2) is obtained by pressure of the exhaust gas on the ambient air. 3) is equivalent to zero mechanical power since the aeroplane is not moving. 4) is independent of the outside air temperature. The combination that regroups all of the correct statements is: 4 2,3 1,2 1,3 Correct Option: 1,3 Refer to figure 021-19. The station numbers before and after the LP turbine are: 4 and 5 6 and 7 3 and 4 1 and 2 Correct Option: 6 and 7 One of the advantages of multiple spool jet engine designs is that: a compressor stall cannot occur under any condition. engine length can be reduced. if one spool seizes, the remaining spool(s) will continue to operate normally. a smaller air starter driving only a single spool can be used. Correct Option: a smaller air starter driving only a single spool can be used. By-pass ratio in a turbine engine is the ratio of the: speed of the combusted air to the speed of the by-pass air. tertiary air mass flow to the primary air mass flow. intake air pressure to the turbine delivery air pressure. cold air mass flow to the hot air mass flow. Correct Option: cold air mass flow to the hot air mass flow. When the combustion gases pass through a turbine stage the: temperature remains constant. pressure increases temperature increases. pressure decreases Correct Option: pressure decreases About a twin spool compressor: When operating at very high RPMs, the later stages within the high pressure spool will have a large negative angle of attack. The low pressure spool runs at a lower RPM than the high pressure spool. Both spools run at the same RPM. The low pressure spool runs at a very high RPM, thus preventing the onset of compressor stall. Correct Option: The low pressure spool runs at a lower RPM than the high pressure spool. Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The speed increases in flow direction. 2) The total pressure increases in flow direction. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. One of the advantages of an annular combustion chamber is: that fewer igniters are required. a simpler assembly. a reduced thermal stress on the turbine. a higher structural strength. Correct Option: a reduced thermal stress on the turbine. Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure rises across the rotor blades. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The static pressure increases in flow direction. 2) The total temperature remains constant in flow direction. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is correct. The characteristics of an axial compressor are: 1) a low pressure ratio by stage. 2) a high pressure ratio by stage. 3) the possibility of compressing a large mass airflow. 4) the inability of compressing a large mass airflow. 1,4 2,3 1,3 2,4 Correct Option: 1,3 The blades in an axial flow compressor decrease in size from the: high pressure to the low pressure stages in order to allow for expansion at increased velocity low pressure to the high pressure stages in an attempt to sustain axial velocity. root to the tip in order to maintain a constant angle of attack root to the tip in order to increase lip clearance. Correct Option: low pressure to the high pressure stages in an attempt to sustain axial velocity. When a gas turbine engine is fitted with can-type combustion chambers: a total of only two igniters are usually fitted since the chambers are inter connected each chamber has its own igniter. each chamber is fitted with two igniters. each chamber is fitted with one igniter and one glow plug. Correct Option: a total of only two igniters are usually fitted since the chambers are inter connected When a gas turbine engine accelerates from idle to take off thrust and the compressor RPM is too high in comparison with the air inlet velocity, this may result in: stagnation.. a compressor stall. supersonic airflow at the compressor blade trailing edges. a compressor blade airflow angle of attack that is too small. Correct Option: a compressor stall. In addition to providing 'customer' bleed for aeroplane systems, air may be bled from compressors of a turbofan engine to increase stall margin: and recover from stall at high rotor speed. at high rotor speed. at low rotor speed. and prevent stall at high rotor speed. Correct Option: at low rotor speed. Static gas temperature decrease when flowing at a subsonic speed through a jet engine exhaust nozzle because the: exhaust nozzle is convergent. velocity of the gas stream decreases. static pressure increases when passing the nozzle. ambient air cools the exhaust casing. Correct Option: exhaust nozzle is convergent. Which of these statements about compressor stall are correct or incorrect? 1) A compressor stall is most likely to occur in a compressor designed for a high pressure ratio operating at low RPM. 2) A compressor stall is most likely to occur when the air flow stagnates in the rear stages of the compressor. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. is correct. 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Compare the pressure ratio of a centrifugal compressor with the pressure ratio of an axial flow compressor. A typical value of the pressure ratio of an axial compressor is: 12:1 35:1 1.2:1 5:1 Correct Option: 35:1 The primary reason for a limitation being imposed on the temperature of gas flow in a gas turbine is to: prevent damage to the jet pipe from overheating. ensure that the maximum acceptable temperature within the combustion chamber is not exceeded. ensure that the maximum acceptable temperature at the turbine blades is not exceeded. prevent overheating and subsequent creep of the nozzle guide vanes. Correct Option: ensure that the maximum acceptable temperature at the turbine blades is not exceeded. A booster or LP compressor in a twin spool turbofan: rotates at the same rotor speed as the IP turbine. rotates at a rotor speed different from both fan, HP compressor and HP turbine. rotates at the same rotor speed as the HP compressor. rotates at the same rotor speed as the fan. Correct Option: rotates at the same rotor speed as the fan. Compare the pressure ratio of a centrifugal compressor stage with the pressure ratio of an axial flow compressor stage. A typical value of the pressure ratio of an axial compressor stage is: 35:1 5.1 12:1 1.2:1 Correct Option: 1.2:1 Which of the following statements regarding variable inlet guide vanes (VIGVs) and variable stator vanes (VSVs) on a high-bypass engines is correct? VIGVs are located in the front of the fan and control both fan and compressor air flow. VSVs are used in the compressor only and are used to increase pressure ratio. If VIGVS move towards the open position, VSVs move towards the closed position. VIGVs are located at the front of the HP compressor and, together with the VSVs, control compressor airflow. Correct Option: VIGVs are located at the front of the HP compressor and, together with the VSVs, control compressor airflow. Each stage of an axial compressor comprises: a row of inlet guide vanes followed by a row of rotor blades. a row of rotor blades followed by a row of inlet guide vanes. inlet guide vanes followed by a row of rotor blades and a row of stator vanes. a row of rotor blades followed by a row of stator vanes. Correct Option: a row of rotor blades followed by a row of stator vanes. The internal geometry of a turbofan intake for a subsonic commercial aeroplane is: divergent in order to reduce airflow velocity and increase static pressure in front of the fan. divergent in order to increase both airflow velocity and static pressure in front of the fan. convergent in order to increase airflow velocity and reduce static pressure in front of the fan. convergent in order to reduce airflow velocity in front of the fan. Correct Option: divergent in order to reduce airflow velocity and increase static pressure in front of the fan. Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The static pressure decreases in flow direction. 2) The total temperature increases in flow direction. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. During the ram recovery process in a gas turbine engine subsonic intake, the: static temperature decreases. flow velocity decreases. total temperature increases. total pressure increases. Correct Option: flow velocity decreases. The primary function of the diffuser, located between the compressor and the combustion chamber, of a gas turbine engine is to: increase the flow velocity of the compressed air. decrease the flow velocity. increase total pressure. convert kinetic energy into mechanical energy. Correct Option: decrease the flow velocity. Turbine blade stages may be classed as either "impulse" or "reaction". In an impulse blade section the pressure across the nozzle guide vanes and ___ across the rotor blades. remains constant, rises remains constant, drops drops; remains constant rises, remains constant Correct Option: drops; remains constant In a gas turbine engine the "creep" phenomenon is the most severe in the: combustor liner. combustor casing. turbine rotors. compressor blades. Correct Option: turbine rotors. One of the advantages of annular combustion chamber over a multiple can is that: only one igniter is required. assembly and disassembly is easier. the thermal load distribution on the HP turbine is more favourable. engine length is shorter because no diffuser is required. Correct Option: the thermal load distribution on the HP turbine is more favourable. Compressor surge may be controlled by means of: fixed inlet guide vanes. a pressure drop control orifice. bleed valves. a variable metering orifice. Correct Option: bleed valves. Compressor stall is due to: an inadequate fuel flow. an excessively low angle of attack of the rotor blades. an excessively high angle of attack of the rotor blades. an excessively high axial velocity Correct Option: an excessively high angle of attack of the rotor blades. A stage in an axial compressor: is made of ring of stator blades followed by a rotor disc. is made of a rotor disc followed by a ring of stator blades. has compression ratio in the order of 2.1. has a compression ratio in the order of 0.8. Correct Option: is made of a rotor disc followed by a ring of stator blades. An axial compressor, when operating below its design speed, has: a tendency to stall in the rear stages. no tendency to stall. a tendency to stall in the front stages. a tendency to stall in the centre stages Correct Option: a tendency to stall in the front stages. The compressor blades in a rotating gas turbine engine are held in position by: a centrifugal latch. abradable seals and aerodynamic forces. the mountings and the resultant of aerodynamic and centrifugal forces. the mountings and thermal expansion only. Correct Option: the mountings and the resultant of aerodynamic and centrifugal forces. Turbine blade stages may be classed as either IMPULSE or REACTION. In an impulse turbine stage: the pressure drops across the stator blades and remains constant across the rotor blades. the pressure remains constant across the stator blades and rises constant across the rotor blades. the pressure remains constant across the stator blades and drops across the rotor blades. the pressure rises across the stator blades and remains constant across the rotor blades. Correct Option: the pressure drops across the stator blades and remains constant across the rotor blades. The turbine in a gas turbine engine generates shaft power using: expansion of hot gas followed by conversion of kinetic energy (velocity) into (mechanical) work. compression of hot gas followed by conversion of potential energy (pressure) into kinetic energy (velocity). expansion of hot gas followed by conversion of potential energy (pressure) into (mechanical) work. compression of hot gas followed by conversion of kinetic energy into (mechanical) work Correct Option: expansion of hot gas followed by conversion of kinetic energy (velocity) into (mechanical) work. Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The speed decreases in flow direction. 2) The total pressure remains constant in flow direction. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is correct. A diffuser in a gas turbine engine is a device in which the: pressure rises at a constant velocity. pressure rises and velocity falls. velocity, pressure and temperature rise. velocity rises and pressure fails. Correct Option: pressure rises and velocity falls. The most unfavourable conditions for adequate inlet air flow of a turbofan engine are: maximum thrust during climb out with strong crosswind. idle thrust, zero forward speed with no wind. maximum thrust at rotation speed on the ground during take off. maximum take off thrust, zero forward speed and strong crosswind conditions. Correct Option: maximum take off thrust, zero forward speed and strong crosswind conditions. In the stator of a turbine, the gas velocity "V" and static pressure "p" vary as follows: V increases; p increases. V increases; p decreases. V decreases; p increases. V decreases; p decreases. Correct Option: V increases; p decreases. The axial compressor of a gas turbine engine usually has more stages than its driving turbine because: bleed air is taken from the compressor. the compressor consumes much more power than the turbine. turbine speed is much higher than compressor speed. the power output of a turbine stage is higher than the power consumption of a compressor stage. Correct Option: the power output of a turbine stage is higher than the power consumption of a compressor stage. The term used to describe a permanent and cumulative deformation of the turbine blades in a gas turbine engine is: creep. stretch. distortion. twist. Correct Option: creep. The purpose of a compressor bleed valve is to prevent surging: at low compressor RPM. generated by foreign object ingestion. with altitude. of the first compressor stages. Correct Option: at low compressor RPM. In a jet engine the propelling nozzle: reduces noise. must be designed to obtain the correct balance of pressure and velocity to prevent flow separation. decreases the velocity and increases the pressure in the jel pipe to provide greater thrust. directs the hot gas stream onto the turbine at the optimum angle of attack... Correct Option: must be designed to obtain the correct balance of pressure and velocity to prevent flow separation. Refer to figure 021-16. A turbine stage is represented by. figure 2. figure 3. figure 1. figure 4. Correct Option: figure 3. Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure drops across the rotor blades. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. is correct. 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is correct. 2) is correct. One of the purposes of the bleed valves fitted to axial flow compressors is to: reduce the tendency to compressor stall. control the acceleration time of the engine spill compressor air should the engine overspeed thus controlling the speed. enable an external air supply to spin up the compressor for engine starting. Correct Option: reduce the tendency to compressor stall. The purpose of variable by-pass valves in a high bypass turbofan engine between the LP compressor and the HP compressor is to: prevent fan surge at high rotor speeds (high rpm). prevent HPC stall during engine start. prevent fan stall and LP compressor stall during engine start and low rotor speeds. provide sufficient air to the HPC during engine start. Correct Option: prevent fan stall and LP compressor stall during engine start and low rotor speeds. Which of these statements about an impulse turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure rises across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure rises across the rotor blades. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Increasing the frequency of variations in thrust setting during normal operation is detrimental to the life of HP turbine blades because: fatigue due to vibration is more severe. creep will be more severe. low cycle fatigue damage is more severe. the EGT limit would be exceeded more often. Correct Option: low cycle fatigue damage is more severe. One advantage of having three instead of two spools in some high-bypass turbofan engine is that: fewer variable stator vanes and variable inlet guide vanes are required to prevent compressor stall at low rotor speeds. fewer variable stator vanes and variable inlet guide vanes are required to prevent compressor stall at high rotor speeds. there is a lower fuel consumption and more thrust due to higher overall pressure ratio. there is a lower fuel consumption due to a higher overall pressure ratio. Correct Option: fewer variable stator vanes and variable inlet guide vanes are required to prevent compressor stall at low rotor speeds. Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure remains constant across the rotor blades. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. is correct. 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. Which of these statements about a reaction turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure rises across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure drops across the rotor blades. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. is correct. 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. is incorrect. 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. The characteristics of a centrifugal compressor are! 1) a high pressure ratio by stage. 2) a large diameter. 3) a low pressure ratio by stage. 4) a small diameter. 1,4 1,2 2,3 3,4 Correct Option: 1,2 Some gas turbine engines have multiple stages in the HP and LP turbines: to help stabilise the pressure between the compressor and the turbine. to reduce noise in the propelling nozzle. because one stage does not generate sufficient shaft power. to facilitate balancing of the turbine assembly. Correct Option: because one stage does not generate sufficient shaft power. The function of the drain valve in a gas turbine combustion chamber is to: remove unburned fuel from the combustion chamber. vent the combustion chamber after engine shutdown. prevent a wet start. check whether the fuel nozzles leak fuel after engine shutdown. Correct Option: remove unburned fuel from the combustion chamber. In which part of a gas turbine engine are high temperature requirements most critical for the materials used? The combustion chamber and exhaust nozzle. The turbine and exhaust nozzle. The last part of the HP compressor and front part of the HP turbine. The combustion chamber and front part of the HP turbine. Correct Option: The combustion chamber and front part of the HP turbine. The purpose of the blow-in-doors at the air inlets is to: serve to increase the relative velocity at the first compressor stage. provide the engine with additional air at high power settings and low air speeds. provide the engine with additional air at high power settings at cruising speed. feed cooling air to the engine cowling. Correct Option: provide the engine with additional air at high power settings and low air speeds. Which of these statements about an impulse turbine are correct or incorrect? 1) The pressure drops across the nozzle guide vanes. 2) The pressure remains constant across the rotor blades. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The dynamic pressure decreases in flow direction. 2) The static temperature increases in flow direction. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. is correct. 2) is incorrect 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Correct Option: 1) is correct, 2) is correct. Creep in a gas turbine engine is: the elastic increase in length of metal parts due to tensile stress and high temperatures. expansion of metal parts due to temperature increase. the permanent increase in length of metal parts due to a combination of tensile stress and high temperatures over a prolonged period. crack formation in metal parts due to cyclic mechanical loads and high temperatures. Correct Option: the permanent increase in length of metal parts due to a combination of tensile stress and high temperatures over a prolonged period. In the axial flow compressor the flow duct is tapered. Its shape is calculated so as to: maintain a constant axial speed in cruising flight. reduce the axial speed, whatever the engine rating. reduce the axial speed in cruising flight. maintain a constant axial speed whatever the engine rating Correct Option: maintain a constant axial speed in cruising flight. Which of these statements about compressor stall are correct or incorrect? 1) A compressor stall is most likely to occur in a compressor designed for a high pressure ratio operating at high RPM. 2) A compressor stall is most likely to occur when the air flow stagnates in the rear stages of the compressor. is correct. 2) is correct. is incorrect. 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. Correct Option: 1) is incorrect. 2) is correct. Refer to figure 021-16. An axial compressor stage is represented by: figure 4. figure 1. figure 3 figure 2. Correct Option: figure 1. Which of the following statements is correct about the flow in a divergent (subsonic) gas turbine engine intake? 1) The dynamic pressure decreases in flow direction. 2) The static temperature decreases in flow direction. 1) is correct, 2) is correct. 1) is correct, 2) is incorrect. 1) is incorrect, 2) is correct. 1) is incorrect, 2) is incorrect. What does the term "EXPECTED APPROACH TIME" mean?
options: The holding time over the radio facility from which the instruments approach procedure for a landing will be initiated.
The time at which ATC expects that an arriving aircraft, following a delay, will leave the holding point to complete its approach for a landing.
The time at which an arriving aircraft, upon reaching the radio aid serving the destination aerodrome, will commence the instrument approach procedure for a landing.
The time at which an arriving aircraft expects to arrive over the appropriate designated navigation aid serving the destination aerodrome.

questions.questionText: What does the term "BROADCAST" mean?
options: A transmission of information relating to air navigation that is not addressed to a specific station or stations.
A radiotelephony transmission from ground station to aircraft in flight.
A transmission containing meteorological and operational information to aircraft engaged in flights over remote and oceanic areas out of range of VHF ground stations.
A transmission where no reply is required from the receiving station.

questions.questionText: What does the term "CLEARANCE LIMIT" mean?
options: The time of expiry of air traffic control clearance.
The time after which air traffic control clearance will be automatically cancelled if the flight has not been commenced.
The time at which aircraft is given an air traffic control clearance.
The point to which aircraft is granted an air traffic control clearance.

questions.questionText: What does the term "BLIND TRANSMISSION" mean?
options: A transmission of information relating to air navigation that is not addressed to a specific station or stations.
A transmission from one station to another station in circumstances where two-way communication cannot be established but it is believed that the called station is able to receive the transmission.
A transmission where no reply is required from the receiving station.
A transmission of messages relating to enroute weather information which may affect the safety of aircraft operations that is not addressed to a specific station or stations.

questions.questionText: What does the abbreviation 'APV' mean?
options: aerodrome precision visibility (monitoring system by observation)

questions.questionText: ICAO defines the term instrument approach as follows:
options: From the beginning of a defined arrival route to a point from which a landing can be completed and thereafter, if a landing is not completed, to a position at which holding or en-route obstacle clearance criteria apply.
From the initial approach fix to a point from which a landing can be completed, e.g. MDA or DA.
From the beginning of a defined arrival route to a point from which a landing can be completed.

From the beginning of a defined approach to a point from which a landing can be completed and thereafter, if a landing is not completed, to an altitude of 1 500 ft above the aerodrome reference point.

questions.questionText: When flying in accordance with IFR, which of the following best describes the term "VISUAL APPROACH"?
options: An approach executed by an IFR flight unable to maintain VMC.
A visual manoeuvre executed by an IFR flight when the weather conditions at the aerodrome of destination are equal to or better than required VMC minima.
An approach by an IFR flight when either part or all of an instrument approach procedure is not completed and the approach is executed in visual reference to terrain.
An extension of an instrument approach procedure to bring an aircraft into position for landing on a runway which is not suitably located for straight-in-approach.

questions.questionText: What rate of speech does ICAO recommend for radio communications? An even rate of speech not exceeding:
Which of the following calls is a "GENERAL CALL"?
What does the term "AIR-GROUND COMMUNICATION" mean?
options: Two-way communication between aircraft and stations or locations on the surface of the Earth.
Any communication from aircraft to ground station requiring handling by the Aeronautical Fixed Telecommunication Network (AFTN).
One-way communication from stations or locations on the surface of the Earth.
One-way communication from aircraft to stations or locations on the surface of the Earth.

questions.questionText: What does the term "WAYPOINT" mean?
options: A specified geographical position used to define an area navigation route or the flight path of an aircraft employing area navigation.
A general term meaning the taxiway and the runway system of an international airport.
A signal indicating the direction of the runway-in-use.
A defined position on an aerodrome used for the calibration of the inertial navigation system.

questions.questionText: What does the abbreviation "MLS" mean?
What does "SELCAL" mean?
options: A system provided for direct exchange of information between air traffic services (ATS) units.
A system in which radiotelephony communication between two stations can take place in both directions simultaneously.
A system which permits the selective calling of individual aircraft over radiotelephone channels linking a ground station with the aircraft.
A system in which radiotelephony communication can be established between aircraft only.

questions.questionText: What does the abbreviation "INS" mean?
What does the abbreviation "RVR" mean?
What does the abbreviation "AIS" mean?
What does the abbreviation "H24" mean?
options: Aircraft handling available 24 hours a day.

questions.questionText: What does the abbreviation "IMC" mean?
What does the abbreviation "SSR" mean?
What does the abbreviation "ATIS" mean?
What does the abbreviation "RNAV" mean?
What does the abbreviation "SID" mean?
What does the abbreviation "SAR" mean?
What does QTE mean?
options: True heading to the station (no wind).

questions.questionText: What does QDR mean?
options: Magnetic heading to the station (no wind).

questions.questionText: What does QDM mean?
options: True heading to the station (no wind).
Magnetic heading to the station (no wind).

questions.questionText: QNH is the Q-code to indicate:
options: the atmospheric pressure at aerodrome elevation (or at runway threshold).
the altimeter sub-scale setting to obtain elevation when on the ground.
the atmospheric pressure measured at the aerodrome reference point (ARP).
the atmospheric pressure referred to the highest obstacle located on the surface of an aerodrome.

questions.questionText: QFE is the RTF Q-code to indicate:
options: the atmospheric pressure referred to the highest fixed obstacle located on the surface of an aerodrome.
the atmospheric pressure corrected to the aircraft cockpit height.
the atmospheric pressure at aerodrome elevation (or at runway threshold).
the altimeter sub-scale setting to obtain the flight level reference datum.

questions.questionText: What is the Q-code for magnetic bearing from the station?
What is the Q-code for magnetic heading to the station (no wind)?
The priority of the instruction "TAXI TO RUNWAY 05" is:
options: less than "CAUTION, CONSTRUCTION WORK LEFT OF TAXIWAY".

questions.questionText: A message concerning aircraft parts and material urgently required is:
The priority of the pilot's message "REQUEST QDM" is:
options: less than "REQUEST CLIMB TO FLIGHT LEVEL..."

questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: There is no difference regarding priority between distress communications and urgency communications.
ATC clearances have the same priority as urgency communications.
The urgency communications have priority over all the other communications, except distress.
The urgency communications have priority over all the other communications.

questions.questionText: The clearance "CLEARED FOR IMMEDIATE TAKEOFF RUNWAY 03" is:
Air traffic control messages (clearances, instructions, etc.) belong to the category of:

options: a message relating to direction finding.

questions.questionText: Which of the messages listed below shall not be handled by the aeronautical mobile service?
A message concerning an aircraft being threatened by grave and imminent danger, requiring immediate assistance is called:
The order of priority of the following messages in the aeronautical mobile service is:
options: direction finding message, distress message and urgency message.
distress message, urgency message and direction finding message.
meteorological message, direction finding message and flight regularity message.
distress message, flight safety message and urgency message.

questions.questionText: The message to an aeronautical ground station "PLEASE CALL A TAXI-CAB FOR US. WE WILL ARRIVE AT 1045 AS SCHEDULED" is:
The order of priority of the following messages in the aeronautical mobile service is:
options: flight safety message, meteorological message, flight regularity message.
flight safety message, direction finding message, urgency message.
meteorological message, direction finding message, flight safety message.
flight regularity message, distress message, meteorological message.

questions.questionText: Flight safety messages are:
options: messages concerning the safety of an aircraft, a vessel, any other vehicle or a person.

options: Climbing flight level two eight zero.

During the transmission of numbers containing a decimal point:
options: the term DECIMAL must always be transmitted.
the term DECIMAL can be omitted with friendly ATS units only.
the term DECIMAL can be omitted if no chance of misunderstanding exists.
the term DECIMAL must be spoken only if followed by three digits.

questions.questionText: During radar vectoring, the controller asks the pilot of an aircraft XY-ABC to turn on to heading 360°. The correct readback of this instruction is:
options: There is no need to respond as you have been identified as XY-ABC.
Heading three hundred and sixty, X-BC.

questions.questionText: What shall the pilot read back for ATC instruction "CLIMB ALTITUDE 2 500 FEET"?
options: Climbing to two thousand five hundred.
Climbing altitude two thousand five hundred feet.
Up to two thousand five hundred.

questions.questionText: What is the correct way of transmitting 1001 as a QNH?

The time is 4:15 PM. What is the correct way of transmitting this time if there is any possibility of confusion about the hour?
When transmitting time, which time system shall be used?
options: No specific system, as only the minutes are normally required.

questions.questionText: What is meant by good microphone technique?
options: Use a normal conversation tone, speak clearly and distinctly.
Speak very loudly into the microphone.
Make large use of hesitation sounds as ER.
Keep the microphone far away since it improves the readability.

questions.questionText: My message will be more effective and understandable if I:
options: maintain the speaking volume at a constant level.

questions.questionText: Before transmitting the pilot should:
options: always write the message and read it during the transmission.
make sure that the aircraft is levelled off.
listen out on the frequency to ensure no interference with another station already transmitting will occur.
make sure that the emergency frequency is tuned in at the same time.

questions.questionText: In the event that a pilot is required to make a "BLIND" transmission, this should be made:
options: only once on the designated frequency.

questions.questionText: What is the consequence of a microphone button stuck on transmit (switched on)?
options: Readability will improve for all stations.
The frequency can not be used by others.
Other stations will have to use the WORD TWICE technique.

questions.questionText: A "BLIND" transmission shall be made:
options: on the designated frequency (frequency in use).

questions.questionText: When an aeronautical station broadcasts information to more than one station, the call starts with:
options: MESSAGE TO ALL AIRCRAFT ON THIS FREQUENCY.

questions.questionText: What does the word "NEGATIVE" mean?
options: Consider that transmission as not sent.

questions.questionText: What does the phrase "BREAK BREAK" mean?
options: The exchange of transmissions has ended and no response is expected.
My transmission has ended and I expect a response from you.
It indicates the separation between messages transmitted to different aircraft in a very busy environment.
It indicates the separation between portions of a message transmitted to an aircraft station.

questions.questionText: What does the word "APPROVED" mean?
options: I repeat for clarity or emphasis.
Authorized to proceed under the conditions specified.

questions.questionText: Which word or phrase shall be used in order to repeat for clarity or emphasis?
ATC clears FASTAIR 345 to descend from FL100 to FL80. What is the correct read back by the pilot?
options: Leaving flight level 100 descending to flight level 80, FASTAIR 345.

questions.questionText: What does the word "DISREGARD" mean?
options: An error has been made in this transmission.
Consider that transmission as not sent.
Wait and I will call you.

questions.questionText: The Air Traffic Control unit has reported "radar contact". What does that mean to the pilot?
options: The aircraft is subject to positive control.
The radar identity of the aircraft has been established.
The pilot does not have to follow up the position of the aircraft.

questions.questionText: Which word or phrase shall be used if you want to say "REDUCE YOUR RATE OF SPEECH"?
You are making an approach to land, at what range would you make the call "SHORT FINAL"?
What does the word "RECLEARED" mean?
options: A change has been made to your last clearance.
Consider that transmission as not sent.
An error has been made in my last transmission.

questions.questionText: What does the word "CANCEL" mean?
options: Wait and I will call you.
Consider that transmission as not sent.
A change has been made to your last clearance.

questions.questionText: Which phrase shall be used if you want to say "YES"?
What does the word "CONTACT" mean?
How shall a pilot inform the control tower that he has to abandon the take-off manoeuvre?
What does the word "CORRECT" mean?
options: Permission for proposed action not granted.
An error has been made in this transmission. The correct version is...

questions.questionText: What does the word "ACKNOWLEDGE" mean?
options: Let me know that you have received and understood this message.
Repeat all of your last transmission.
Repeat all of this message back to me exactly as received.

questions.questionText: If you are flying an aircraft with a maximum rate of climb of 1 500 ft per minute and ATC asks you to climb at 2000 ft/min or greater, your answer will be:
Which word or phrase shall be used to indicate that a change has been made to your last clearance and this new clearance supersedes your previous clearance or part thereof?

options: Fastair 345 should listen on frequency 118.9 on which TOWER will initiate further communications.
Fastair 345 should standby on the current frequency.
Fastair 345 should change frequency to 118.9, on which aerodrome data are being broadcast.

questions.questionText: Which phrase should a pilot use to inform ATC that he is initiating a missed approach procedure?
Which of these phrases is used if you want to communicate that a message "CONSIDER THAT TRANSMISSION AS NOT SENT"?
Which elements of a position report cannot be omitted?
What does the word "MONITOR" mean?
options: Wait and I will call you.

questions.questionText: When shall the phrase "TAKEOFF" be used by a pilot?
options: To inform TOWER when ready for departure.
Only when the aircraft has already moved onto the active runway.
Never, it is used only by the control tower.

questions.questionText: Which word shall be used to ask a station whether you have correctly received a message, clearance, instruction, etc?
ATC reports traffic. Which phrase should be used if a pilot wants to inform ATC that he/she does not see the corresponding traffic?
To indicate that he is no longer occupying the active runway a pilot shall report to the ATC controller.

FASTBIRD 166, REPORT RUNWAY 24 IN SIGHT, WIND 270 DEGREES 15 KNOTS.
FASTBIRD 166, REPORT FINAL RUNWAY 24, WIND 360 DEGREES 18 KNOTS.

questions.questionText: ATC reports traffic. Which phrase should be used if a pilot wants to inform ATC that he/she can see the corresponding traffic?

options: Indicate weather conditions as wind, visibility, temperature.
Indicate whether you are flying in IMC or in VMC..
Indicate if visibility is sufficient for landing
Indicate whether you are flying IFR or VFR.

questions.questionText: Which word or phrase shall be used when giving authorization to proceed under specified conditions?
What does the word "WILCO" mean?
options: I have received all of your last transmission.
I will call you later - communication is difficult.
I understand your message and will comply with it.

questions.questionText: Which word shall be used to indicate that an error has been made in a transmission or message?
If ATC asks aircraft D-ENRW to standby, what is the pilot expected to reply, if any?
options: No reply necessary, ATC will call back

questions.questionText: What does the word "CHECK" mean?
What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station indicating approach control radar departures?
What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station providing surface movement control of aircraft on the manoeuvring area?
What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station providing approach control (no radar service)?
What is the radiotelephony call sign suffix for the aeronautical station indicating aerodrome control service?
What is the radiotelephony call sign suffix for the aeronautical station indicating clearance delivery?
What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station indicating Area Control Centre (no radar)?
What is the radiotelephony call sign suffix for the aeronautical station providing radar service (in general)?
What is the radiotelephony call sign for the aeronautical station indicating approach control radar arrivals?
An Airbus 321 registered as G-HBTD has call sign "Sun Air HBTD". What is its abbreviated call sign?
When shall an aircraft in the heavy wake turbulence category include the word HEAVY immediately after its call sign e.g. FASTAIR 345 HEAVY?
options: in all calls to the aerodrome tower and the approach control unit.
in the initial call to the aerodrome control tower and the approach control unit.

questions.questionText: When is an aircraft station allowed to use its abbreviated call sign?
options: After it has been addressed in this manner by the aeronautical ground station.
Only after satisfactory communication has been established.
Provided no confusion is likely to result.

questions.questionText: Which of the following abbreviated call signs of aircraft XY-ABC is correct?
What, if any, is the abbreviated call sign of "FASTAIR 2345"?
What is the correct call sign of "FASTAIR 345" in the initial call to the aerodrome control tower and the approach control unit, if the aircraft has a maximum take-off weight of 136 tonnes?
